text:
Once upon a time on a very unusual island, every night there was a parade celebrating the most beautiful flower that never blooms. It had an aura that made people want to stay awake just to get a whiff of it.

The flower itself was tiny, only a few centimeters wide—but it emitted such a sweet fragrance that you could travel miles just to inhale a few breaths of it.

Every Saturday evening the citizens would go out into the grasslands surrounding the island's main village.

They would each carry one small pot with them.

You never knew whose bouquet would blossom next.

When it bloomed, you’d have a day off. You'd wander around, let it drift slowly into your nose, let its sweet scent swim across your brain.

You could imagine its petals drifting across continents—it could tell endless stories...

You could almost hear its voice

Every time you petted it

It would giggle softly

Like the wind through dandelions

You'd spend your afternoon sitting
